name: Measured Golf
address: 2019 W Stadium Blvd, Ann Arbor, MI 48103
about: Measured Golf is a golf training and performance facility offering expert instruction through science and technology. The venue provides golfers of all levels with access to advanced tools like Trackman 4 launch monitors, Foresight Sports Quad Max, 3-angle HD video analysis, and Swing Catalyst dual motion plates for comprehensive swing and game improvement. Coaching sessions are available in various durations, from 50-minute lessons to full-day programs, and can be conducted in-person or online. The facility is designed for serious practice, featuring a 3,000 sq. ft. short game area with a sod wall bunker and a biomechanics lab, all within a climate-controlled environment. With a focus on data-driven coaching, Measured Golf caters to players looking to make measurable improvements to their game.
why_golfers_like_it: Access to premium, multi-faceted technology including Trackman, Foresight Sports, and Swing Catalyst for detailed swing analysis
Comprehensive coaching options tailored to individual goals, from 50-minute sessions to full-day programs and online coaching
Specialized biomechanics lab for advanced ground reaction force analysis, making it ideal for performance-focused players

simulator_hardware.notes: Premium launch monitor with 3 hitting bays
Dual motion plates for ground reaction force analysis

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: Yes, you are expected to bring your own golf clubs to use during your session at the facility.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drink?
faq.answer: The venue does not mention food or drink services, so bringing your own may be allowed but is not specified.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The facility is focused on training and practice rather than simulating full rounds of golf, so 18-hole times are not applicable.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, Measured Golf offers coaching programs for juniors and youth, making it a suitable environment for young golfers to learn and improve their skills.
